calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions [Probate Code Section 9611]
Off Calendar Sep-25-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Katz for and with Keith Cameron (Administrator).

The Court noted that this is a petition for instructions seeking to now distribute to a special needs trust, that the concern is that a final order was issued almost a year ago and that distribution was made to Dawn on settlement. Mr. Katz stated that yes there was a final judgment, that no distribution was made, that some of the blame falls on his shoulders, that statutes under Probate Codes 3600 don't apply and that under Probate Code 9611 the Court has general authority. Mr. Katz also stated that Ms. Cameron is clearly disabled and not capable of caring for herself, that her health has deteriorated quite significantly, that she is close to death, and that she has consented to the special needs trust, but is not capable of creating it. In addition, Mr. Katz stated that Ms. Cameron's mental faculties have deteriorated and that no one is going to object. The Court questions why distribution wasn't made at the time the order was issued, stated that it does not know that it has any jurisdiction at this point, and that there are other mechanisms available to create and fund a special needs trust. The Petition for Instructions is denied.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions [Probate Code Section 9611]
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Continued To Sep-25-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-20-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nicholas Jellins for and with Keith Cameron.
Dawn Cameron (Beneficiary) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Cameron stated that she wants it to be over with, that it's destroying her family, that the Attorney hasn't done anything for her and that she does not want to take the time to speak with another Attorney. Ms. Cameron also stated that she is not exactly satisfied with the terms of the settlement, but she agrees to it, and that she does not feel she is being forced into this. The Court is satisfied that Dawn Cameron is voluntarily entering into the settlement/stipulation for entry of order and is prepared to do so without the advise of Counsel. The Court granted the Amended First and Final Account and Report of Administrator and Petition for Its Settlement, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-20-2017 Continued To Jul-11-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nicholas Paul Jellins for Keith Cameron; James Bridgman in the interest of Dawn Cameron

Mr. Bridgman stated his concern that the substitution of attorney removing him as counsel for his former client Dawn Cameron was the result of undue influence from her brother Keith Cameron. He is alarmed that without appropriate legal representation his former client will be deprived of her rightful inheritance. Mr. Jellins objected to Mr. Bridgman appearing on behalf of a client who had terminated his representation. He stated that courtesy copies of the amended petition papers had been provided to the Court in April, and that an additional copy was delivered today. He informed the court that he has obtained and will be filing a stipulation between Keith Cameron and Dawn Cameron providing for distribution of the estate as provided in the amended petition. He requested that the hearing be continued to allow the filing of the stipulation. Mr. Bridgman responded that he had never been provided with the stipulation, and objected that Mr. Jellins had apparently been in contact with his client Dawn Cameron behind his back during his representation. The matter was continued to July 11, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Jan-30-2017 Continued To Feb-15-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nicholas Jellins appeared by telephone for Keith Cameron (Petitioner/Administrator).
James Bridgman for Dawn Cameron (Beneficiary).

Mr. Jellins stated that escrow closed December 22, 2016, that they are preparing a petition for final distribution and that he expects to have it filed this week. Mr. Bridgman stated that an accounting and inventory are needed, and that he would like to have it coupled with distribution. Mr. Jellins stated that the inventory was filed in April of 2014. Status on sale of real property is continued to February 15, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Duong. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Dec-19-2016 Continued To Jan-30-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for (objector/beneficiary) Dawn Cameron. Nicholas Jellins (via Court Call) for (administrator) Keith Cameron.

The Court asked for an update. Mr. Jellins reported that the property is in escrow, that it is expected to close this week, that a notice of proposed action was served and no objections received, that the contract has been ratified and they are ready to close. Mr. Bridgman asked if there is a Tenants in Common (TIC) agreement in place. Mr. Jellins stated that he is not informed that one has been reached, that it was a contingency of the original contract and that it may have been waived or confirmed. The Court stated that the lack of a TIC was an issue. Mr. Jellins replied that it had been previously, that it was not an issue with this buyer and that he expects to file a petition for final distribution within 45 days. Mr. Bridgman stated that his client has been without a place to live for 9 months and that he does not know why it is going to take 45 additional days. Mr. Jellins stated that it is the administrator's intent to close as soon as possible, that if Ms. Cameron waives notice it will happen sooner and that they will try to have it filed by January 30, 2017. The status on sale of real property was continued to January 30, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Oct-31-2016 Continued To Dec-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for beneficiary Dawn Cameron; Nicholas Jellins for trustee Keith Cameron.

Mr. Jellins stated that there is no TIC agreement in place, and the proposed sale of the property fell through when the buyer's 1031 exchange fell through. The property is now back on the market. Mr. Bridgman said that his client has concern over the delay in sale of the property; she is without a home and the sale is not proceeding in an expedited manner as agreed. The matter was continued to December 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a further status update.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Continued To Oct-31-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for (objector) Dawn Cameron.

The Court stated that Mr. Jellins is not present. Mr. Bridgman gave a history of the case, stated that the brother is the administrator, that he evicted Ms. Cameron to sell the house, that it is a Tenant in Common (TIC) situation, that there is no agreement and that nothing has been done. The Court stated that per the examiner's notes efforts have been made regarding the TIC agreement. Mr. Bridgman stated that every time they are here there is representation made stating that the sale is going to happen, that he has had no time to communicate with Mr. Jellins and that he will renew their petition. The Court stated that a report on the administration has been filed and that the sale is proceeding. Mr. Bridgman stated that his client is living on couches of friends based on the sale and asked that the Court issue an Order to Show Cause regarding what is going on. The Court stated that it will issue an Order to Appear to Mr. Jellins. The status on sale of real property was continued to October 31, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Subsequent to the hearing the Clerk from Department 305 reported that Mr. Jellins had been on Court Call for that department instead of department 204. The Court will not issue the Order to Appear, and Mr. Jellins will be notified of the new date.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition To Remove Administrator Keith Cameron And Appointment Of Dawn Cameron As Sole Administrator Probate Code 8500, 8502; Issuance Of Letters; For Breach Of Uty And Fiduciary Duty
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for and with Dawn Cameron. Nicholas Jellins for and with Keith Cameron.

Mr. Bridgman stated that there has been no progress in the case, that his client has moved out of the house, that there is no indication that the City & County of San Francisco was ever told that the parents had died, that there is no bank account established, that the bills associated with the house are in Ms. Cameron's name with the exception of the mortgage, that there is no TIC in place, that the neighbor knows nothing about it and that they do not believe that the present administrator should continue on. Mr. Jellins stated that the allegations in the petition are false, that the other allegations are irrelevant, that petitioner has done all he can to administer the estate, that Ms. Cameron had refused to vacate the property, that they had to have the Sheriff's department get her out, that the stipulation to vacate was breached, that there were defects in the title that had to be cleared, that a real estate agent has been obtained and is present in court, that they are getting the property ready to sell, that it is the only asset of the estate, that it can now be placed on the market, that the petition for removal was in bad faith and that the costs of responding should be assessed against Ms. Cameron's distribution. Mr. Bridgman stated that there is $12,000.00 in rent that is missing, that it is the duty of the administrator to pay the bills and to establish a bank account and that the Sheriff did not come to remove his client from the property. The Court took the petition to remove administrator Keith Cameron and appointment of Dawn Cameron under submission, proposed orders are to be submitted by the end of this week. A status on the sale of the real property is set for June 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Remove Administrator Keith Cameron And Appointment Of Dawn Cameron As Sole Administrator Probate Code 8500, 8502; Issuance Of Letters; For Breach Of Uty And Fiduciary Duty
Off Calendar Apr-04-2016 Continued To Apr-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for and with (petitioner) Dawn Cameron. Nicholas Jellins for (administrator) Keith Cameron.

The Court stated that it is in receipt of the response by Mr. Jellins and that a date needs to be set for a reply. Mr. Bridgman stated that Mr. Cameron has been avoiding service, that he did not appear for his deposition and that he should be suspended and the court should issue an Order to Show Cause. Mr. Jellins stated that that is unnecessary, that he is not avoiding service, that they have been trying to communicate with Mr. Bridgman for 5 months to no avail, that they filed a responsive pleading, that in regards to the deposition he was not served, that they had no rights to conduct discovery, that the only issue before the court today is removal, that Ms. Cameron is trying to avoid removal from the estate property, that they had a UD trial in January and the parties stipulated to possession, that the property was to be vacated and turned over by 4/3/16 and that if she has not vacated the property they will file a writ of possession. The Court asked if the citation had been served. Mr. Jellins replied yes and that they had interposed objections that he was not a party to the action, that he had not been served, that they have now waived the defects and that the delay was occasioned by Ms. Cameron not vacating the property. The Court asked if there was any issue with taking the deposition now. Mr. Bridgman stated that it had been properly served at the residence listed in the court's file, that Mr. Cameron has not provided either them or the court with a good address, that the property needs work in order to be ready for sale, that there has been no movement by the administrator to get this done, that Ms. Cameron is willing to cooperate and that vacant property in San Francisco is not a good thing. The Court continued the petition to remove administrator Keith Cameron and appoint Dawn Cameron as sole administrator, etc., to April 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the reply due by April 20, 2016, and told the parties to work out a date for the deposition. Mr. Bridgman asked the court to issue a stay. The Court stated that it is not before the court and that it should be taken up in Dept. 501. Mr. Jellins stated that it is the administrator's intent to go forward with the eviction. Mr. Bridgman replied that it is an abuse of the probate process.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

Verified Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Oct-06-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nicholas Jellins for and with (petitioner) Keith Cameron. Cecilia Kwan Ng (50% owner) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that there is an issue with this not being brought on behalf of the full ownership, that it does not think it is an 850 issue, that it is an Unlawful Detainer (UD) issue to gain possession of the occupied property and that there is a specific statutory way to do this. Mr. Jellins stated that he has reviewed the examiners notes, that he has filed the supplemental petition, that he has communicated with Ms. Ng regarding her interest in the property, that they were uncertain the court would allow this procedure, that the co-owner is inhabiting the estate property and that there is no agreement regarding occupancy between the administrator and the heir. The Court stated that the UD procedure is designed to be a quick and efficient means of testing possession and that this petition cannot go forward. Mr. Jellins stated that the petition for instructions askes whether to proceed on an 850 or a UD. The court stated that it is up to the administrator as to what step to take, that this petition needs to go off calendar and that they can proceed as they see necessary. Ms. Ng stated that she only received the document on Friday and did not have time to hire an attorney. The petition for instructions was taken off calendar without prejudice to other actions or petitions.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)
